'''Liam Hood''' (born 6 January 1992) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He began his career with the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], where he also spent time on loan at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] and the [[Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Championship 1]]. After a season with the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League, and a short stay at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the Championship, Hood joined the [[Leigh Centurions]]. Here he spent six years in the top two divisions, across two spells, separated by a brief stint with the [[Widnes Vikings]]. He later played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] for four years before joining Castleford in 2026.

== Background ==
Hood was born in [[Bradford]], West Yorkshire, England.

Having progressed through the [[Leeds Rhinos]] academy system, Hood was promoted to the first-team squad prior to the 2012 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 November 2010 |title=Rhinos promote academy quartet |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/6498479/rhinos-promote-academy-quartet |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

== Career ==
=== Leeds Rhinos ===
In September 2011, Hood signed a four-year contract with Leeds alongside fellow hooker [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=28 September 2011 |title=Leeds Rhinos give McShane and Hood new contracts |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/15086733 |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made his Super League debut on 2 March 2012 against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], scoring a late try.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 March 2012 |title=Castleford 14-36 Leeds |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17195468 |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Later on in the season, he was loaned to [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] where he made twelve appearances, scoring twice.

He was granted a release from Leeds in September 2013, seeking further game time.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gordon |first=James |date=6 September 2013 |title=Hood released by Rhinos |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hood-released-by-rhinos |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Hunslet Hawks ===
In 2013, Hood played for [[Hunslet Hawks|Hunslet]] in the [[RFL Championship]] through their dual registration arrangement with Leeds. Although they suffered relegation to the [[Championship 1]], he signed a one-year deal to join the club on a permanent basis in 2014.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 January 2014 |title=Former Leeds hooker Hood joins Hunslet |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-leeds-hooker-hood-joins-hunslet |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Across two seasons, he made thirty-six appearances and scored eighteen tries.

=== Salford Red Devils ===
In August 2014, Hood agreed a two-year deal to join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] from the [[2015 Salford Red Devils season|2015 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 August 2014 |title=Salford Red Devils: Liam Hood joins Super League club for 2015 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28802997 |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=15 August 2014 |title=Super League: Salford sign former Rhino Liam Hood |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/9422578/super-league-salford-sign-former-rhino-liam-hood |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He made twenty-two appearances, scoring one try. He was one of several Salford players to be released at the end of 2015.<ref name="BBC Salford release">{{Cite web |date=19 November 2015 |title=Salford Red Devils: Liam Hood and Luke Menzies released |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34868902 |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== Swinton Lions ===
After leaving Salford, Hood pulled out of a proposed move to join Burdekin Roosters in the [[Townsville District Rugby League]] in Australia.<ref name="BBC Salford release" /> He instead joined [[Swinton Lions]] in the Championship for 2016, though made only four appearances before departing in March.<ref name="Sky Swinton Leigh">{{Cite web |date=9 March 2016 |title=Leigh sign hooker Liam Hood from Swinton on two-year deal |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12199/10198032/leigh-sign-hooker-liam-hood-from-swinton-on-two-year-deal |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

=== Leigh Centurions ===
In March 2016, [[Leigh Centurions]] announced the signing of Hood on a two-year deal from Championship rivals Swinton.<ref name="Sky Swinton Leigh" /> He made nineteen appearances and scored four tries as the Centurions won promotion to the top flight via the [[2016 Super 8s#The Qualifiers|Super 8s]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 September 2016 |title=The Qualifiers: Leigh Centurions 48-40 Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/37287178 |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 8 June 2017, Hood scored two tries from dummy-half as Leigh recorded their first win against rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 33 years.<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 June 2017 |title=Leigh 50-34 Wigan: Eight-try Centurions shock Warriors |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leigh-vs-wigan/46089 |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He played in the 2017 [[Million Pound Game]] defeat to [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=30 September 2017 |title=Million Pound Game: Leigh Centurions 10-26 Catalans Dragons |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41402866 |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In October, Hood signed a new two-year deal to remain with Leigh as they returned to the Championship.<ref>{{Cite web |date=30 October 2017 |title=Liam Hood: Leigh Centurions hooker signs new two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41808949 |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Hood scored seventeen tries in twenty-two appearances throughout 2018, including braces against [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]], [[London Broncos|London]], and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].<ref name="RLRKC" />

=== Widnes Vikings ===
In October 2018, Hood signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] on a one-year contract ahead of their [[2019 Widnes Vikings season|2019 season]] in the Championship.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=22 October 2018 |title=Widnes sign Leigh star Liam Hood |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-sign-leigh-star-liam-hood |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=22 October 2018 |title=Liam Hood: Widnes sign Scotland international hooker from Leigh Centurions |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45945696 |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He registered just four appearances, scoring two tries, before being granted a release for personal reasons after the club entered administration.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 March 2019 |title=Liam Hood becomes latest player to leave Widnes |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/11655299/liam-hood-becomes-latest-player-to-leave-widnes |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

=== Return to Leigh Centurions ===
Shortly following his Widnes exit, Hood rejoined Leigh in March 2019, this time on a part-time basis.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gordon |first=James |date=5 March 2019 |title=Widnes bring in Dom Speakman as Leigh confirm Liam Hood return |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-bring-in-dom-speakman-as-leigh-confirm-liam-hood-return |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

Hood was named Leigh vice-captain for the 2020 season,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Critchley |first=Mike |date=15 January 2020 |title=Danny Addy is a true leader for Leigh |url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/18162792.danny-addy-true-leader-leigh/ |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Leigh Journal}}</ref> and had scored five tries in six games at the time of the Championship's [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league|suspension due to COVID-19]]. In August 2020, Hood signed a new contract with Leigh, committing to the club for the 2021 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=11 August 2020 |title=Adam Walker joins Leigh as Liam Hood signs new deal with Centurions |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/adam-walker-joins-leigh-as-liam-hood-signs-new-deal-with-centurions |access-date=22 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/blog/2020/08/10/leigh-centurions-confirm-re-signing-of-liam-hood/|title=Leigh Centurions Confirm Re-Signing Of Liam Hood|website=Leigh Centurions|accessdate=19 December 2020|date=10 August 2020|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Hood was appointed Leigh captain for the 2021 campaign on their return to Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=5 March 2021 |title=Liam Hood to captain Leigh in return to Super League |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/liam-hood-to-captain-leigh-in-return-to-super-league |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=15 December 2020 |title=Leigh Centurions hooker Liam Hood delighted to get another shot at Super League |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leigh-centurions-hooker-liam-hood-delighted-to-get-another-shot-at-super-league-3067673 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

=== Wakefield Trinity ===
On 17 August 2021, Hood signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a two-year deal from the [[2022 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=17 Aug 2021|accessdate=13 Oct 2021|title=Wakefield Trinity sign Tom Lineham and Liam Hood for 2022 Super League season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/12383542/wakefield-trinity-sign-tom-lineham-and-liam-hood-for-2022-super-league-season}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=8 December 2021 |title=Wakefield Trinity: Why new signing Liam Hood feels he's finally 'arrived' in Super League |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-why-new-signing-liam-hood-feels-hes-finally-arrived-in-super-league-3487274 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

Hood signed a one-year contract extension with Wakefield in January 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 January 2023 |title=Hood signs extension with Trinity |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3240/hood-signs-extension-with-trinity |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> He made twenty-one appearances for Wakefield Trinity in [[2023 Super League season|2023]] as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated, ending their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=Sky Sports|date=16 September 2023|access-date=24 February 2026}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=16 April 2023 |title=Wakefield Trinity's Liam Hood knows all about relegation dogfights as he looks to rescue season |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-leigh-leopards-rugby-29722508 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=The Mirror}}</ref> 

In October 2023, he became the first player to commit to Wakefield's new regime in the Championship by agreeing a new contract.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=24 October 2023 |title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos man Liam Hood signs new Wakefield Trinity contract under coach Daryl Powell |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ex-leeds-rhinos-man-liam-hood-signs-new-wakefield-trinity-contract-under-coach-daryl-powell-4384083 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=24 October 2023 |title=New Wakefield Trinity boss Daryl Powell expresses delight after retaining Liam Hood |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/new-wakefield-trinity-boss-daryl-powell-expresses-delight-after-retaining-liam-hood-4384079 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He made twenty-nine appearances and scored twelve tries in their promotion campaign.<ref name="RLRKC" />

Head coach [[Daryl Powell]] praised Hood's strong form in the opening months of the 2025 season, though said he was unlikely to be retained due to salary cap management.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=10 March 2025 |title=Wakefield Trinity likely to let star performer leave as Daryl Powell explains reason why |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/wakefield-trinity-likely-star-performer-1022208 |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Baxter |first=Trevor |date=27 March 2025 |title=Liam Hood prepares for Leigh return with Wakefield Trinity |url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/25044030.liam-hood-prepares-leigh-return-wakefield-trinity/ |access-date=24 February 2026 |website=Leigh Journal}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 2 October 2025, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Hood on a one-year deal with the option of a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 October 2025 |title=Castleford sign hooker Hood from Wakefield |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ckgz4r0r3pwo |access-date=2 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2 October 2025 |title=Hood joins the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article/67/hood-joins-the-tigers |access-date=2 October 2025 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 9 for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his debut against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 1 against [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lanigan |first=Adam |date=15 February 2026 |title=Wigan start with hard-fought win at Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c363nd47400o |access-date=16 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In March, he underwent surgery for an ongoing knee injury and missed three matches.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=25 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers suffer latest injury blow as key man undergoes surgery |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-suffer-latest-injury-blow-as-key-man-undergoes-surgery |access-date=8 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On return in round 8, Hood sustained a significant neck injury in the first half of Castleford's win against Wigan;<ref>{{Cite web |date=29 April 2026 |title=Castleford's Hood out with 'significant' neck injury |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4p744zwrxo |access-date=8 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> he completed the full match without reporting any symptoms, however was hospitalised the following morning and ruled out of action for twelve weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=29 April 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers star played on with horror neck injury as lengthy absence likely |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-star-played-on-with-horror-neck-injury-as-lengthy-absence-likely |access-date=8 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

== International career ==
Liam made his international début for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|2012 European Cup]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

In October and November 2015, Liam played for Scotland in the [[2015 European Cup]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
